Professional Investment Thesis: Primary beneficiary of the low-carbon transition. With a 100% EAF share and best-in-class intensity (0.43 tCO2/t), Nucor is effectively 'future-proofed' against carbon taxes, allowing for significant market share capture as competitors' margins erode.

Primary beneficiary of the low-carbon transition. With a 100% EAF share and best-in-class intensity (0.5 tCO2/t), Steel Dynamics is effectively 'future-proofed' against carbon taxes, allowing for significant market share capture as competitors' margins erode.

Operational efficiency is high, but heavy reliance on traditional BF-BOF routes in Europe/Global creates a transition bottleneck. While currently stable, ArcelorMittal requires an estimated $34.8B in green capex to avoid long-term structural margin compression (> 29.1%).

High-risk exposure to intensifying carbon regulations. With a carbon intensity of 2.1 tCO2/t, Baowu Steel faces a terminal threat to profitability, with base-case margin erosion exceeding 37.1%. The lack of aggressive H2-DRI transition plans makes the current valuation unsustainable.
